question_title: Will another company get dropped from the Dow Jones Industrial Average in July or August 2026?
summary: The question requires a NEW announcement, confirmation, or credible report between June 30 and Sept 1, 2026 that one of the 30 current DJIA components will be removed. S&P Dow Jones Indices just completed a swap (Alphabet in, Verizon out, effective June 29, 2026) — the 54th change since 1896 — and rarely makes back-to-back changes within a 2-month window absent a forcing corporate event. No scheduled quarterly rebalance falls in July-August (next is September). Speculation about Nike being replaced by Berkshire Hathaway is real but framed as 'within 12 months' and consists of opinion pieces, not credible reports of a decided removal. Status quo strongly favors NO.

factors.description: S&P just made a change (Alphabet/Verizon, effective June 29); back-to-back changes within 2 months are rare
No scheduled quarterly S&P rebalance in July-August window (June done, next is September)
Active media speculation about Nike removal raises some chance of a credible report
Speculation is 'within 12 months' and consists of opinion pieces, not confirmations of a decided removal

reasoning:
**Forecast: 8% (No)**

The question needs a fresh announcement or credible report between June 30 and September 1, 2026 that one of the 30 current DJIA components will be removed. Key reasoning:

- **S&P just acted**: The Alphabet/Verizon swap (effective June 29) was this cycle's change. S&P Dow Jones Indices almost never makes back-to-back changes within two months absent a forcing corporate event (merger/acquisition).
- **No scheduled window**: The next quarterly rebalance is September, outside the July-August window.
- **Speculation isn't confirmation**: The Nike/Berkshire chatter is real but framed as "within 12 months" and lives in opinion pieces — not credible reporting of a decided removal.
- **Status quo dominates**: A random 2-month window carries ~15% base rate, materially reduced given a change just occurred and no rebalance is due.

Final probability: **0.08**
sources_consulted: [Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DJIA)

resolution_criteria:
This question resolves as **Yes** upon any official announcement or confirmation or [credible source ](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)reporting after June 30, 2026 and before September 1, 2026 that one of these companies will no longer be a component of the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) index:

3M 
Alphabet
American Express
Amgen
Amazon
Apple
Boeing
Caterpillar
Chevron
Cisco
Coca-Cola
Disney
Goldman Sachs
Home Depot
Honeywell
IBM
Johnson & Johnson
JPMorgan Chase
McDonald's
Merck
Microsoft
Nike
Nvidia
Procter & Gamble
Salesforce
Sherwin-Williams
Travelers Companies
UnitedHealth Group
Visa
Walmart

If no such announcement or reporting occurs before September 1, 2026, this question resolves as **No**.
fine_print:
Although removals from the DJIA are generally effective immediately in order to forestall frontrunning, this is not a requirement for this question. All that is required is for there to be an announcement or confirmation before in the time listed that a company will be removed from the index at any point in time. Therefore this question will resolve based on the announcement or confirmation, not on the actual removal date.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/44244) which opened on 2026-06-26 18:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.

factors.description: DJIA changes are rare; S&P just made a change (Alphabet/Verizon effective June 29) and rarely makes back-to-back changes within 2 months
No scheduled quarterly S&P rebalance in July-August window (June done, next is September)
Active speculation about Nike removal, raising some probability of a credible report
Speculation framed as 'within 12 months,' not imminent; predictions are opinion pieces, not confirmations

input.anchor: ~15% base rate for a DJIA change announcement in a random 2-month window (roughly one change/year in recent era), reduced because a change just occurred
input.assessment: Question needs a NEW announcement/confirmation/credible report between June 30 and Sept 1, 2026 that one of 30 current DJIA components will be removed. S&P Dow Jones Indices just completed a swap (Alphabet in, Verizon out) effective June 29. They almost never make consecutive changes within 2 months absent M&A. No scheduled rebalance falls in the window. Speculation about Nike exists but is explicitly 'within 12 months' and consists of opinion pieces, not credible reports of a decided removal. Base rate for a change announcement in a random 2-month window is ~15%, but conditional on a change just occurring, it drops materially. Estimate ~7-8%.
input.calibration_notes: Status quo strongly favors NO. Not hedging near 50 — data supports low estimate. Speculation is priced as visibility, not a decided event.
input.tool_audit: web_search returned useful recent articles confirming Alphabet/Verizon change and Nike speculation framing. FRED gave current DJIA levels (not central to this). No failures.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward definitional/predictive question. Scaffolding fine; no subforecast needed given clear low base rate. Main judgment is whether opinion-piece speculation counts as 'credible source reporting' — it doesn't for a decided removal.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified all four factors against the research trace and checked resolution-criteria alignment, the pre-publication event handling, anchor divergence, counterargument quality, and the tool audit. No forecast-relevant errors found.

FACTORS — all grounded:
- Factor 1 (Alphabet/Verizon swap effective June 29; back-to-back changes rare): directly confirmed by multiple trace sources (Motley Fool, cryptobriefing, AOL). ✓
- Factor 2 (no scheduled rebalance in July-Aug, next September): reasonable inference from S&P's quarterly cadence (Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec); no explicit source line but standard general knowledge, not a hallucination. ✓
- Factors 3 & 4 (Nike speculation framed 'within 12 months,' opinion pieces not confirmations): confirmed verbatim by the Motley Fool/AOL/cryptobriefing articles in the trace. ✓
No double-counting (factors draw on distinct claims: base rate, rebalance schedule, Nike catalyst, speculation framing). Logit signs are all directionally correct (rarity/no-rebalance negative; speculation positive; opinion-piece framing negative).

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Criteria are provided and require a NEW announcement/confirmation/credible report AFTER June 30 and before Sept 1 that a listed company WILL be removed. The agent correctly engages the specific terms — distinguishing 'credible report a company will be removed' from opinion-piece speculation, and correctly noting the low resolution bar.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: The Alphabet/Verizon swap (announced ~June 23, effective June 29) predates both the window opening (June 30) and publication (July 3). This is a Case-2 situation, and the agent handles it correctly — it does NOT treat the swap as resolving YES; it uses it only as base-rate evidence (back-to-back changes are rare), building the forecast on forward-looking probability. Correct.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate is ~7-12%. Unconditional base rate ~15% for a 2-month window; reduced by (a) a change having just occurred, (b) no scheduled rebalance in-window, and (c) the required 'credible report of a decided removal' bar being materially higher than the existing 'within 12 months' opinion pieces. The agent's 8% sits inside my range — no meaningful divergence.

ANCHOR: 15% → 8% is a modest ~7pp reduction, well under the 20pp threshold and justified by the just-occurred/no-rebalance logic. Fine.

COUNTERARGUMENT: Strong and genuine — constructs a coherent alternative (post-swap media frenzy + Nike catalyst + Berkshire eligibility + low resolution bar → 20%+), not a token restatement.

SELF-CONFIDENCE (0.80): Appropriately calibrated for a well-researched low-probability event.

TOOL AUDIT: Agent claims 'no failures' and cites FRED for DJIA levels; ground truth shows 8 calls, 0 errors, fred_series 1 call — consistent.

MINOR (non-blocking): The agent's summary response mentions 'Barron's' as a source, which does not appear in the trace (only Motley Fool/AOL/cryptobriefing/MSN). Trivial unsupported detail; the underlying conclusion (Nike speculation in opinion pieces) is well-grounded and forecast direction is unaffected.
